issues.title: Focus gets lost on Android TV
issues.body:
Run the code below. It displays two raised buttons and a text field and another raised button.

You can use the DPad (or the cursor keys if trying this on the simulator) to change the focus between both buttons. I changed their focus color to make this very obvious.  You can active buttons with RETURN. You cannot activate them using the SELECT button on the DPad but that's something I can fix myself and but part of the bug report.

However, once you focus the text field, you cannot get your focus back and the app ist stuck until you stop and start it again. A hot restart is not enough to free the focus.

Once the text field has the focus, the keyboard opens. On the simulator, it has a DONE button. Selecting that button (don't press ENTER, that's cheating) closes the keyboard – but only if the text field has no `onEditingComplete` callback! As a workaround, overriding `onSubmitted` is triggered on DONE and then the keyboard still closes.

I would expect that `onEditingComplete` also works.

Adding `f.requestFocus();` to the `onSubmitted` callback (`f` is the focus node of the third raised button) is a workaround to get the focus back:

```dart
TextField(
  onSubmitted: (s) => f.requestFocus();
),
```

If I now again enter the text field, this warnings are printed:
```
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): getTextBeforeCursor on inactive InputConnection
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): getTextBeforeCursor on inactive InputConnection
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): getTextAfterCursor on inactive InputConnection
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): deleteSurroundingText on inactive InputConnection
W/IInputConnectionWrapper( 4205): commitText on inactive InputConnection
```
Something is wrong here.

My expectation is that the text field works as with a native Android app:

* when focused, it is displayed as "highlighted" but no keyboard opens
* when selected while focused, the keyboard opens, you can edit until DONE is selected and the keyboard closes while the text field stays focused
* while focused, you can use UP to move to the previous button (after moving the cursor to the beginning)
* while focused, you can use DOWN to move to the next button (after moving the cursor to the end)
* while focused, you can use LEFT and RIGHT to move the cursor. 


```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: MyHomePage(),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  final f = FocusNode();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            RaisedButton(
              focusColor: Colors.yellow,
              onPressed: () => print("1"),
              child: Text("Hallo!"),
            ),
            RaisedButton(
              focusColor: Colors.amber,
              onPressed: () => print("2"),
              child: Text("Hallo!"),
            ),
            SizedBox(
              width: 200,
              child: TextField(
                onSubmitted: (s) {
                  print(s);
                  f.requestFocus();
                },
              ),
            ),
            RaisedButton(
              focusNode: f,
              focusColor: Colors.lime,
              onPressed: () => print("3"),
              child: Text("Hallo!"),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!--
      Run your application with `flutter run --verbose` and attach all the
      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an

comments: The problem is NOT _"You cannot activate them using the SELECT button on the DPad"_ because _"that's something I can fix myself and but part of the bug report."_ (as demonstrated with your snippet) but it is _"However, once you focus the text field, you cannot get your focus back and the app ist stuck until you stop and start it again."_

Hi,
Did anyone found a solution to this?
Focus lost when submit textfield and i can't move anything. have idea ?
up, i have same issue
I'm facing similar issue, once i focus the text field on android tv, i cannot get the focus back and the app is stuck until i stop and start it again.. any solution on this please?

Maybe related? https://www.reddit.com/r/flutterhelp/comments/jxahj7/need_your_help_five_days_of_searching_for/

The user upgraded Flutter and got this issue as well and solved it by downgrading to stable 1.20.2.

>

I think it is better to draw a page directly, even if it is 1.20.2, I still have bugs here.

Is downgrading the version still the only solution? Any other work around? 
I am on flutter 1.22.5 and this is still a problem, I can't downgrade because I have packages that depend on the latest version of flutter

it is not convenient for me, as my users are allowed to change the keyboard language. your approach doesn't work for me

I can confirm these issues on latest Flutter master version
having the same issue. are there any news on that topic?
same issue +1
@TahaTesser any updates?
Have the same issue. Need to understand how flutter's focus system works - [https://flutter.dev/docs/development/ui/advanced/focus](https://flutter.dev/docs/development/ui/advanced/focus)
same problem, any solution?

It's not a great solution, but it is a solution. I programmed my own keyboard and stored the input in a TextField. TextField's parametrs are readOnly: true, enabled: false so TV cannot access it and cannot get freezed. It works and I didn't have to change the version of Flutter.

> It's not a great solution, but it is a solution. I programmed my own keyboard and stored the input in a TextField. TextField's parametrs are readOnly: true, enabled: false so TV cannot access it and cannot get freezed. It works and I didn't have to change the version of Flutter.

Could you share this keyboard? Thank you :)

Thanks for Sharing. But with this you lose system features like voice typing which is really useful with a TV, different languages...

I hope after more Thant 2 years we will get a fix downgrading to flutter v1.20.2 is not an option 😅
i'm having the same problem any updated solutions??
@bypass112 how do you actually focus on the text input? pressing on Select will do that automatically?

> @bypass112 how do you actually focus on the text input? pressing on Select will do that automatically?

I have autofocus turned on for the TextField, other than that I get focus on it through keypad navigation.

It is still the problem.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/74042801/fix-focus-for-controls-on-android-tv

Hello @akucherk 

I tested my solution on flutter 3.10.0 and can confirm that it works.

Below are the files I used, feel free to contact me if you still have any problem with this.


[focus_fix.zip](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/11521083/focus_fix.zip)

@akucherk Look at your keyboard, It's like popup window, so viewInsets.bottom is 0 even the keyboard is opened , where @abrachid is like android keyboard their viewInsets.bottom value will change when opening keyboard, That is the issue in your case. @abrachid solution will not get approved by google play app submit, I have faced this already, better you to implement custom keyboard and textinput for you.

@akucherk Focus issue is the main problem for google play approve, The real issue is in google tv the keyboar appearing on over the app like popup without affecting app screen size from bottom (Like in mobile it will change the app screen height from bottom), That is the main reason we can't able to detect when keyboard is opening and when dismissing , If we can detect the dismissial of keyboard then we can programatically move the focus that is the problem in flutter.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-framework` label.
Guys, did you have difficulty typing something on the native Google TV keyboard?
